vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1747: bug(roles): roles/ship.md does not exist but every ship spawn passes --agent roles/ship.md

plan.title: Finish the PAN-1531 ship-role removal: stop spawning the vestigial ship agent and reconcile docs
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: The reactive Cloister scheduler maps test.passed -> shipping -> role 'ship' and spawns an interactive ship agent with `--agent roles/ship.md`, but roles/ship.md does not exist. The agent is instruction-less (buildReactiveRolePrompt points it at the same missing file) and its --agent flag references a missing path, which silently flips the permission-posture branch in getRoleRuntimeBaseCommand. docs/ROLES.md and CLAUDE.md both document roles/ship.md as a real instruction source. This is the surfaced tip of the unfinished PAN-1531 ship-role removal: docs/MERGE-WORKFLOW.md (the authoritative design) says the interactive ship role was retired and the rebase + readyForMerge flip are server-side, yet the live code still spawns a now-vestigial ship agent and runs a dedicated deacon patrol (checkUndispatchedShip) to re-fire it.
narratives.Proposal: Finish the PAN-1531 removal (direction B'), NOT author roles/ship.md (direction A). PAN-1650 already made readyForMerge event-driven server-side (review-status.ts:258-272 derives it from gate state on every setReviewStatus write), so the reactive ship agent is vestigial -- removing it is safe with no liveness regression and no new server-side flip needed. (1) Stop spawning the ship agent: ROLE_RUN_STATES.shipping -> null while keeping the shipping STATE for display/flywheel. (2) Remove the deacon checkUndispatchedShip safety-net patrol. (3) Make agent spawn honest: verify a role definition file exists before passing --agent. (4) Reconcile docs and stale in-code comments. Keep 'ship' as the merge-specialist identity (router['role:ship']->merge_agent, roleForSpecialist('merge')->'ship', merge activity source:'ship') and a historical-agent-state token -- do NOT mass-excise it; that would break merge model routing and activity attribution.

autoDecisions.summary: Chose direction B' (finish the PAN-1531 ship-role removal) over direction A (author roles/ship.md), and over the issue's two original fix-directions.
autoDecisions.rationale: docs/MERGE-WORKFLOW.md (the authoritative PAN-1531 design) already retired the interactive ship role for stated reasons (LLM creative rebase resolutions changed semantics; redundant gates; extra orchestration cost). PAN-1650 then made readyForMerge event-driven server-side, so the reactive ship agent is verified vestigial. Reintroducing an LLM ship agent (direction A) reverses a deliberate decision; making roleAgentDefinitionPath('ship') null while still spawning an instruction-less agent (issue direction B) is strictly worse. Aligning the live code to the authoritative doc is the defensible choice.

autoDecisions.summary: Scoped the removal to the reactive ship spawn + deacon patrol + honest spawn + docs; KEPT 'ship' as the merge-specialist identity token.
autoDecisions.rationale: 'ship' is entangled with the 'merge' specialist: router['role:ship'] routes to the merge_agent model (router-config.ts:96), roleForSpecialist('merge')/roleForSpecialistModel('merge') return 'ship' (specialist-context.ts:108, specialists.ts:34), merge-agent.ts emits source:'ship' activity, and isSpecialistRole includes 'ship'. Mass-excising 'ship' from the Role union/contracts/frontend would break merge model routing and merge activity attribution. The bug is fully resolved without excision.

autoDecisions.summary: Did NOT build a new server-side readyForMerge flip.
autoDecisions.rationale: PAN-1650 (review-status.ts:258-272) already derives readyForMerge from gate state on every setReviewStatus write, so it flips true the instant review+test+verification pass -- before the ship agent even spawns. The owner's comment premise that 'the ship run flips readyForMerge / is load-bearing' predates PAN-1650 and is no longer true. Removal alone is safe.

autoDecisions.summary: Deferred removing 'ship' from ADVANCING_ROLES, reap-terminal-sessions, and the reserved-advancing-slots accounting.
autoDecisions.rationale: Those touch the concurrency budget and are dead-but-harmless once ship never spawns (no ship sessions exist to budget for or reap). Touching DEFAULT_RESERVED_ADVANCING_SLOTS risks a budget regression disproportionate to this bug. Left intact; flagged as future cleanup.

items.title: Stop the reactive scheduler from spawning a ship agent (ROLE_RUN_STATES.shipping -> null)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/service.ts, change ROLE_RUN_STATES.shipping from 'ship' to null so stateToRole('shipping') returns null and onIssueStateChange('shipping') becomes a no-op (no ship agent spawned). Remove 'ship' from the spawnable-role list in roleFromAgentId (~line 179). Keep the test.passed -> 'shipping' state transition in issueStateChangeFromDomainEvent unchanged -- only the role spawn is removed; the shipping STATE remains for frontend phase display and flywheel MERGE_GATE_VERBS. Update src/lib/cloister/__tests__/reactive-scheduler.test.ts to assert no ship agent is spawned on test.passed.

subItems.title: A test.passed domain event no longer spawns an agent-<issue>-ship session; onIssueStateChange('shipping') logs 'no role for issue state shipping' and returns.
roleFromAgentId no longer lists 'ship' among spawnable roles.
reactive-scheduler.test.ts updated to assert shipping produces no spawn; the suite is green.
The shipping STATE transition still occurs (issueStateChangeFromDomainEvent unchanged); frontend 'Shipping' phase and flywheel MERGE_GATE_VERBS are unaffected.

items.title: Remove the deacon checkUndispatchedShip safety-net patrol

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts remove the checkUndispatchedShip() function (~3097-3160), the SHIP_DISPATCH_STALENESS_MS / SHIP_DISPATCH_COOLDOWN_MS constants (~3066, 3070), the shipDispatchCooldowns / shipDispatchCounts maps (~3071-3072), and the patrol-loop call site + action aggregation (5174-5176). Delete the dedicated test src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-undispatched-ship.test.ts -- the behaviour it guards is gone. Leave checkReadyForMergeStuck (PAN-344) and checkFailedMergeRetry intact: those are the real post-readyForMerge merge-trigger safety nets and are unrelated to ship dispatch.

subItems.title: checkUndispatchedShip and its SHIP_DISPATCH_* constants and cooldown/count maps are removed; grep finds no remaining references.
The deacon patrol loop no longer calls checkUndispatchedShip (lines 5174-5176 removed); typecheck and lint pass with no dead vars/imports.
deacon-undispatched-ship.test.ts is removed; the full test suite is green.

items.title: Make agent spawn honest: verify a role definition file exists before passing --agent

narrative.Action: In src/lib/agents.ts getRoleRuntimeBaseCommand (~464-503), before emitting `--agent <definitionPath>`, verify the resolved role definition file exists on disk (resolve relative to the path claude loads --agent against -- the workspace/project root). When the file is absent, take the no-definition branch: apply the global Claude permission flags (getClaudePermissionFlagsStringSync), skip bypassPrefixForAgentFlagSync, omit --agent, and log a single warning. Keep this generic (an existence check for any role) rather than special-casing ship to null, so no future role can silently point --agent at a missing file. This is the issue's third explicit requirement. Add a unit test.

subItems.title: When the resolved role definition file is missing, getRoleRuntimeBaseCommand emits NO --agent flag and includes the global permission flags (no-definition branch).
When the file exists, behaviour is unchanged: --agent <path> is emitted, permissionFlags is empty, and the bypass prefix is applied.
A single warning is logged when a role definition file is missing (the failure is no longer silent).
A unit test covers both the missing-file and present-file branches.

items.title: Reconcile ship-role docs and stale in-code comments to the post-removal reality

narrative.Action: Update docs and stale in-code comments to match the post-removal reality. In docs/ROLES.md remove the `ship | roles/ship.md` row (~lines 17-34) and the ship entry in the new-role checklist context (~138); state that shipping is server-side (rebaseFeatureBranch + PAN-1650 event-driven readyForMerge) with no spawned ship role, and that 'ship' survives only as the merge-specialist identity. In CLAUDE.md update the role-taxonomy table (~line 112) to drop roles/ship.md as the ship instruction source. Add a one-line note in docs/MERGE-WORKFLOW.md that the reactive 'shipping' state no longer spawns any agent. Update stale in-code comments that describe ship as a spawned 'final pipeline stage': src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts:320, src/dashboard/server/routes/command-deck.ts:558-561, src/dashboard/server/routes/specialists.ts:1083 and :720.

subItems.title: docs/ROLES.md no longer lists roles/ship.md as an existing instruction source; the ship row reflects server-side shipping + merge-specialist identity.
CLAUDE.md role-taxonomy table no longer cites roles/ship.md.
docs/MERGE-WORKFLOW.md notes that the reactive shipping state spawns no agent.
Stale in-code comments in projects.ts, command-deck.ts, and specialists.ts no longer describe ship as a spawned pipeline stage.
npm run lint (incl. scripts/lint-skills.sh) still passes -- no doc/skill drift introduced.
